---
title: "Themes and Plot Colouring"
format: html
---

## APA-formatted figures

There are two approaches here: using a purpose-built theme from an existing package, or building the APA look manually with `theme()`. Both are worth knowing.

**Packages required:**

-   ggplot2 (part of **tidyverse**)

-   jtools [@jtools]

### Using the theme_apa() function in ggplot2

``` r
#| eval: false
ggplot(data, aes(x = condition, y = outcome)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(x = "Condition", y = "Outcome Score") +
  theme_apa()
```

**What this is doing:**

-   `theme_apa()` applies a pre-built theme designed to match APA figure conventions in one line — removing gridlines, using a clean black-and-white base, and adjusting font/line styling to match APA's expectations for published figures.

::: callout-tip
## Tip

`theme_apa()` has a few optional arguments worth knowing: `legend.pos = "none"` removes the legend entirely (useful if your plot doesn't need one), and `facet.title.size` adjusts facet label sizing if you're using `facet_wrap()`/`facet_grid()`. Run `?theme_apa` for the full list, and visit this cookbook for the full ggplot2 customisation: [@Chang].
:::

### Building the APA look manually

These are the APA conventions specified manually in ggplot2:

``` r
#| eval: false
ggplot(dat, aes(x = condition, y = outcome)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(x = "Condition", y = "Outcome Score") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )
```

::: callout-tip
## Tip

You can apply these APA formatting conventions to any kind of ggplot2 figure: just add the `theme()` part to the end of the plot code.
:::

## Colour blindness

Use the **viridis** colour package for **ggplot2**. This has colours designed to be seen regardless of colour blindness.

More information available here: [https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/scale_viridis.html](#0)
